goal: Extend `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` so the `PluginManager` can (a) hot-reload a registered plugin process WITHOUT restarting the daemon — draining in-flight requests via a per-runtime `tokio::sync::RwLock<()>` gate before swapping the child — and (b) trip a circuit breaker after `MAX_RESTARTS = 3` consecutive failed restart attempts, with exponential backoff (base 1 s) between attempts, transitioning the runtime to `PluginState::Error`. Two new acceptance tests at the module root: `plugin_manager::test_hot_reload` (P2-W6-F03) and `plugin_manager::test_circuit_breaker` (P2-W6-F04). No other crate, no other module, no fixture changes.
plan_summary: Three changes in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` plus a re-export tweak in `lib.rs`. (1) Per-runtime in-flight gate: add `pub in_flight: Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<()>>` to `PluginRuntime` (default `Arc::new(RwLock::new(()))`); document that callers acquire the read half for the duration of a tool call so the writer-side `reload` can drain. Add `pub restart_attempts: u32` (default 0) to `PluginRuntime` for the circuit-breaker counter. (2) Hot-reload: add `pub async fn PluginManager::reload(&mut self, name: &str) -> Result<(), PluginError>` — looks up the runtime by name; clones a snapshot of `manifest` + `in_flight`; drops the manager's read lock; acquires the writer half of the runtime's `in_flight` lock (this DRAINS readers); calls `Self::health_check(&manifest)` to spawn the new child + perform the MCP handshake; on success re-acquires the manager's write lock to flip the runtime's `state = Active`, `last_call = Instant::now()`, `restart_attempts = 0` and emit one `tracing::info!(target: "ucil.plugin.lifecycle", op = "reload", plugin = %name, ...)` event. Returns `PluginError::NotFound { name }` if no runtime with that name is registered. The writer guard is dropped before the function returns so post-reload calls can re-acquire the read half. (3) Circuit breaker: add `pub const MAX_RESTARTS: u32 = 3` and `pub const CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BASE_BACKOFF_MS: u64 = 1000`; add `pub async fn PluginManager::restart_with_backoff(&mut self, name: &str) -> Result<(), PluginError>` — loop up to `MAX_RESTARTS` times, calling `Self::health_check(&manifest)` per attempt; on success: reset `restart_attempts = 0`, mark Active, return Ok; on failure: increment `restart_attempts` then `tokio::time::sleep(base * 2u32.pow(attempt))` between attempts (1 s, 2 s, 4 s with the default base; customisable per-test via the new `with_circuit_breaker_base(d: Duration)` builder). When the loop exhausts MAX_RESTARTS without success, call `runtime.mark_error(format!("circuit breaker tripped after {N} restart attempts"))` and return a new `PluginError::CircuitBreakerOpen { name, attempts }` variant. Plus: add `pub fn PluginManager::add(&mut self, runtime: PluginRuntime)` so callers (and the new acceptance tests) can register a pre-built runtime without going through `activate` (which requires a successful health check). Plus: extend `lib.rs` re-exports with `MAX_RESTARTS` and `CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BASE_BACKOFF_MS`.
scope_in: crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — extend `PluginRuntime` struct with two new public fields: `pub in_flight: Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<()>>` (default `Arc::new(RwLock::new(()))`) and `pub restart_attempts: u32` (default `0`). Update `PluginRuntime::new` and `PluginRuntime::discovered` constructors to initialise both. Update the existing `test_hot_cold_lifecycle` struct literal at lines ~1188-1205 (and any other `PluginRuntime { ... }` literals inside `mod tests {}`) so the additive struct fields keep the test build green — do NOT delete or reorder existing field initialisers (cumulative WO-0042 lesson).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — add two new public constants beside `HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS` / `DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES`: `pub const MAX_RESTARTS: u32 = 3` (master-plan §14.2 default) and `pub const CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BASE_BACKOFF_MS: u64 = 1000` (1 s base for exponential backoff per the F04 description). Each constant carries one rustdoc paragraph citing the master-plan source.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — add `circuit_breaker_base: Duration` field to `PluginManager` (default `Duration::from_millis(CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BASE_BACKOFF_MS)`). Add a `pub fn PluginManager::with_circuit_breaker_base(mut self, base: Duration) -> Self` builder so the `test_circuit_breaker` acceptance test can shrink the backoff to ~5 ms and complete inside ~50 ms instead of taking 7 s of wall-clock time. The default constructor `PluginManager::new()` continues to use the 1 s production base — no behavioural change to existing call sites.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — add `pub fn PluginManager::add(&mut self, runtime: PluginRuntime)` that pushes a pre-built `PluginRuntime` onto `self.runtimes`. Returns nothing. Existence rationale: callers (and the F04 acceptance test) need to register a runtime WITHOUT going through `activate` which requires a successful health check against a real MCP child. This is the natural complement to the existing `activate(&mut self, manifest: &PluginManifest)` API and matches the future `ucil plugin install <name>` flow that will land in P2-W6-F07. The method is a one-liner (`self.runtimes.try_write().ok()?` shape — implementer's choice between `try_write` panic-on-contention or async `write().await` blocking).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — add `pub async fn PluginManager::reload(&mut self, name: &str) -> Result<(), PluginError>`. Behaviour spec: (1) snapshot the runtime by name — clone `manifest` and `in_flight: Arc<RwLock<()>>`; release the manager's read lock immediately. Return `PluginError::NotFound { name: name.into() }` if no runtime with that name. (2) Acquire the WRITER half of the runtime's `in_flight` lock — this BLOCKS until every outstanding read-guard (i.e. every in-flight tool call) has been dropped. Hold the writer guard for the entire reload window. (3) Call `Self::health_check(&manifest_clone)` — this spawns a fresh child, runs the full MCP handshake, kills the child, returns the tools list. (4) On success: re-acquire the manager's WRITE lock, locate the runtime by name, set `state = PluginState::Active`, `last_call = Instant::now()`, `restart_attempts = 0`. (5) On failure: leave `state` unchanged, increment `restart_attempts`, propagate the error. (6) Drop the writer guard before returning so the next tool call's `acquire_in_flight()` can proceed. Emit one `tracing::info!(target: "ucil.plugin.lifecycle", op = "reload", plugin = %name, "plugin reload complete")` event on success and one `tracing::warn!(target: "ucil.plugin.lifecycle", op = "reload", plugin = %name, error = %e, "plugin reload failed")` on error.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — add `pub async fn PluginManager::restart_with_backoff(&mut self, name: &str) -> Result<(), PluginError>`. Behaviour spec: (1) snapshot the manifest by name; return `PluginError::NotFound { name: name.into() }` if absent. (2) Loop `attempt in 0..MAX_RESTARTS`. Each iteration: call `Self::health_check(&manifest)`; on Ok — re-acquire the write lock, set `state = Active`, `last_call = Instant::now()`, `restart_attempts = 0`, log a `tracing::info!(target: "ucil.plugin.lifecycle", op = "restart_with_backoff", plugin = %name, attempts = %(attempt + 1), "plugin restart succeeded")` and return Ok. On Err — re-acquire the write lock, increment `restart_attempts`, drop the lock, then `tokio::time::sleep(self.circuit_breaker_base * 2u32.pow(attempt))` (i.e. base × {1, 2, 4} for the three attempts). (3) After exhausting MAX_RESTARTS attempts with no success: re-acquire the write lock, locate the runtime, call `runtime.mark_error(format!("circuit breaker tripped after {} restart attempts", MAX_RESTARTS))`. Return `PluginError::CircuitBreakerOpen { name: name.into(), attempts: MAX_RESTARTS }`. (4) Tracing: emit one `tracing::warn!(target: "ucil.plugin.lifecycle", op = "restart_with_backoff", plugin = %name, attempts = %MAX_RESTARTS, "plugin circuit breaker tripped")` event when the breaker opens.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — extend `PluginError` with two new variants under the existing `#[non_exhaustive]` enum: (a) `PluginError::NotFound { name: String }` with `#[error("plugin `{name}` is not registered with this manager")]`, and (b) `PluginError::CircuitBreakerOpen { name: String, attempts: u32 }` with `#[error("plugin `{name}` circuit breaker tripped after {attempts} restart attempts")]`. Both errors carry owned data (no `&'static`) since the plugin name is dynamic.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — module-root `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_hot_reload()` (NOT in `mod tests { }`). Spawns the existing `mock_mcp_plugin_path()` mock, calls `mgr.activate(&manifest)` to register a runtime in `Active`, then in a background `tokio::spawn` task acquires the runtime's `in_flight.read()` guard and sleeps ~100 ms before dropping it. From the main task: `let start = Instant::now(); mgr.reload("hot-reload-fixture").await.expect("reload"); let elapsed = start.elapsed();`. Assertions: (a) `elapsed >= Duration::from_millis(100)` — proves the writer waited for the in-flight reader. (b) `elapsed < Duration::from_secs(2)` — sanity bound. (c) `mgr.registered_runtimes().await[0].state == PluginState::Active`. (d) `restart_attempts == 0`. (e) `last_call > start` — the reload bumped the timestamp. (f) Re-acquire `in_flight.read()` after the reload returns — must succeed (writer guard was released). The test does NOT install a global tracing subscriber.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — module-root `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_circuit_breaker()` (NOT in `mod tests { }`). Builds a `PluginManifest` with `transport.command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_breaker_binary__"` (any path that ENOENTs). Constructs a runtime via `PluginRuntime::new(manifest)`; registers it via the new `mgr.add(runtime)`. Configures the manager via `let mut mgr = PluginManager::new().with_circuit_breaker_base(Duration::from_millis(5));` so backoff is {5, 10, 20} ms instead of {1, 2, 4} s. Calls `let result = mgr.restart_with_backoff("breaker-fixture").await;`. Assertions: (a) `matches!(result, Err(PluginError::CircuitBreakerOpen { name, attempts }) if name == "breaker-fixture" && attempts == MAX_RESTARTS)`. (b) `mgr.registered_runtimes().await[0].state == PluginState::Error`. (c) `restart_attempts == MAX_RESTARTS` (i.e. 3). (d) `error_message.as_deref().is_some_and(|m| m.contains("circuit breaker"))`. (e) Total `elapsed >= Duration::from_millis(35)` (5+10+20 = 35 ms minimum) — proves backoff occurred. (f) Total `elapsed < Duration::from_secs(2)` — fast-test invariant. The test does NOT spawn the mock-mcp-plugin (no real subprocess required). The test does NOT install a global tracing subscriber.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — extend rustdoc on `PluginRuntime`, `PluginManager`, the new `reload` / `restart_with_backoff` / `add` methods, and the new `MAX_RESTARTS` / `CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BASE_BACKOFF_MS` constants. Keep additions SHORT — the crate is under `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` and prior phase-1 WOs (WO-0009/0024/0025/0027/0039) were rejected for shorthand intra-doc links. Use plain backticks or fully qualified `[Self::method]` paths only.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs — extend the existing `pub use plugin_manager::{ ... }` block (lines 105-109) to add `MAX_RESTARTS` and `CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BASE_BACKOFF_MS`. Keep the alphabetical convention: insert in the constants tail of the list. Does NOT touch any other re-export. Rationale: the WO-0042 lessons-learned post explicitly flagged the deferred lib.rs re-export pattern — these new public constants will be needed by P2-W6-F07 (`ucil plugin` CLI) when it wires `--max-restarts` flag display, so we surface them now to avoid the same deferral debt.

crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml — NO new deps required. `tokio` already exposes `sync::RwLock`. `tracing` is in `[dependencies]`. If the executor needs a dev-dep that is not already pulled in (rare), it lands under `[dev-dependencies]` only with a one-line commit-message rationale. Default expectation: NO Cargo.toml change.

Commit ladder — approximately 6 commits, each ≤50 LOC except the test-introduction commits per DEC-0005: (1) `feat(daemon): extend PluginRuntime with in_flight gate + restart_attempts counter` (~30 LOC field + ctor updates + struct-literal fixups in `mod tests {}`); (2) `feat(daemon): add MAX_RESTARTS + CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BASE_BACKOFF_MS constants and PluginManager backoff config` (~25 LOC consts + builder + field); (3) `feat(daemon): add PluginManager::add to register pre-built PluginRuntimes` (~15 LOC); (4) `feat(daemon): add PluginManager::reload with in-flight drain + tracing` (~50 LOC reload + NotFound error variant); (5) `feat(daemon): add restart_with_backoff + circuit-breaker trip semantics` (~55 LOC restart loop + CircuitBreakerOpen error variant); (6) `feat(daemon): re-export MAX_RESTARTS + CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BASE_BACKOFF_MS from lib.rs` (~3 LOC); (7) `test(daemon): add plugin_manager::test_hot_reload acceptance test` (~80 LOC; over-budget per DEC-0005 module-root test commit precedent); (8) `test(daemon): add plugin_manager::test_circuit_breaker acceptance test` (~70 LOC; same DEC-0005 precedent). Soft target ≤50 LOC/commit; the test commits MAY exceed.
scope_out: NO changes to ANY other crate: `ucil-core`, `ucil-treesitter`, `ucil-cli`, `ucil-lsp-diagnostics`, `ucil-embeddings`, `ucil-agents`. The CLI surface (`ucil plugin reload`) is feature P2-W6-F07 and lives in a sibling WO.
NO new module, NO renamed module — every change lands inside `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` and the existing `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` (re-export-only edit).

NO changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs`, `server.rs`, `executor.rs`, `session_manager.rs`, `session_ttl.rs`, `storage.rs`, `startup.rs`, `priority_queue.rs`, `text_search.rs`, `understand_code.rs`, `watcher.rs`, `test_support.rs`, `main.rs`. The reload + circuit-breaker work is purely inside `plugin_manager.rs`; consumers (notably `server.rs`'s `tools/call` handler) are NOT yet wired to `acquire_in_flight()` — that wiring is a future WO scoped to the plugin-call-routing feature.

NO changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manager.rs`, `tests/e2e_mcp_stdio.rs`, `tests/e2e_mcp_with_kg.rs`, `tests/support/**` — frozen integration suites + the mock-mcp-plugin binary stay untouched. The new acceptance tests are MODULE-ROOT in `src/plugin_manager.rs` per DEC-0007.
NO ast-grep / Probe plugin manifests, NO `ucil plugin` CLI, NO plugin-lifecycle integration suite. Those are P2-W6-F05 / F06 / F07 / F08 in sibling work-orders.
NO new manifest sections — the plugin TOML schema landed in WO-0042 and is frozen for this WO.

NO HOT/COLD lifecycle changes — the existing `tick` / `mark_call` / `with_idle_timeout` / `wake` / `activate` surface is preserved untouched so the frozen `test_hot_cold_lifecycle` (P1-W3-F06) keeps passing.

NO changes to the existing state-machine transitions (`register` / `mark_loading` / `mark_active` / `stop` / `mark_error`) introduced in WO-0042. The two new operations (`reload`, `restart_with_backoff`) compose existing transitions; they do NOT add new variants to `PluginState`.

NO new binary in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/bin/` — the breaker test deliberately uses a non-existent path so `Self::spawn` returns `PluginError::Spawn { source: ENOENT }`, exercising the failure branch without a second mock binary.

NO global tracing-subscriber install in either new test (avoids cross-test contamination — WO-0042 lesson). Both tests assert state via the public `state` / `error_message` / `restart_attempts` fields.
NO changes to `Cargo.toml` `[workspace.dependencies]` table at the repo root. Default expectation: ZERO changes to any `Cargo.toml`.
NO `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, commented-out assertions, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.
NO `git commit --no-verify` / `--amend` after push / force-push — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.

Mutation check (verifier runs): stash the body of `PluginManager::reload` so it returns `Ok(())` without acquiring the writer lock or calling `health_check` → `test_hot_reload` fails on the `elapsed >= 100ms` assertion (the in-flight reader is never drained). Restore → green. Proves `reload` is the sole reason the assertion holds.

Mutation check (verifier runs): stash the body of `PluginManager::restart_with_backoff` so it returns `Ok(())` immediately → `test_circuit_breaker` fails on the `Err(PluginError::CircuitBreakerOpen { .. })` match (the runtime never trips). Restore → green. Proves `restart_with_backoff` is the sole reason the breaker-trip assertion holds.

Provides the `register` / `mark_loading` / `mark_active` / `stop` / `mark_error` transitions and the `error_message` field that `restart_with_backoff` writes when the breaker trips.

lessons_applied: DEC-0007 frozen-selector placement: both new acceptance tests land at the MODULE ROOT of `src/plugin_manager.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests { }`) so nextest reports them under `plugin_manager::test_hot_reload` / `plugin_manager::test_circuit_breaker` — exactly the selectors frozen in feature-list.json. Same pattern as the existing `test_hot_cold_lifecycle` / `test_manifest_parser` / `test_lifecycle_state_machine` (WO-0042 carry-forward).

WO-0042 lesson — additive struct fields require updating every literal in `mod tests {}`: this WO adds `in_flight: Arc<RwLock<()>>` and `restart_attempts: u32` to `PluginRuntime`; the executor MUST update every `PluginRuntime { ... }` struct literal inside the file (the existing `test_hot_cold_lifecycle` builds one at lines ~1188-1205) so the test build stays green. WO-0042 hit this exact issue with `error_message` and called it out as the top lesson for the next state-machine WO.

Pre-baked mutation checks per WO-0042 lesson `For planner` line 80: `acceptance` includes two explicit verifier-runs-mutation entries (one per feature) naming the specific function bodies to stash (`PluginManager::reload` body, `PluginManager::restart_with_backoff` body). Verifier does not have to invent the mutation patch.

Backward-compat regression guards: acceptance_criteria explicitly runs the existing `test_hot_cold_lifecycle`, `test_manifest_parser`, `test_lifecycle_state_machine`, `--test plugin_manager`, `--test e2e_mcp_stdio`, `--test e2e_mcp_with_kg` — six explicit Phase-1 / Phase-2-prior-WO regression sub-checks. WO-0042 introduced this discipline; this WO carries it forward.

Single-file blast radius: the entire WO touches one source file (`crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs`) plus a 2-symbol re-export tweak in `lib.rs`. Forbidden-paths list is correspondingly tight (35 entries — same template as WO-0042 plus `crates/ucil-daemon/src/bin/**` since this WO does NOT add a second mock binary).

Rustdoc link discipline: prior phase-1 rejections (WO-0009 / WO-0024 / WO-0025 / WO-0027 / WO-0039) all stemmed from `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` triggering on a shorthand link. This WO keeps rustdoc additions SHORT and uses plain backticks or fully qualified paths only. Acceptance criterion runs `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps`.

Cargo-test summary-line regex with alternation (WO-0038/0039 lesson): acceptance_criteria uses `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. ... 0 failed|... tests run: ... passed'` matching BOTH cargo-test and cargo-nextest shapes.

WO-0042 lesson — explicit `pub use` for new public types: the new constants `MAX_RESTARTS` and `CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BASE_BACKOFF_MS` are added to the existing `pub use plugin_manager::{ ... }` block in `lib.rs` — NOT deferred to a follow-up WO. Avoids the deferred-export debt that WO-0042 left for P2-W6-F07.

No-mocks-of-critical-deps invariant from CLAUDE.md: `test_hot_reload` spawns a REAL `mock-mcp-plugin` subprocess (just like the existing `test_hot_cold_lifecycle`); the breaker test deliberately uses a non-existent path so `Self::spawn` returns `PluginError::Spawn { source: ENOENT }`, exercising the failure branch without mocking `tokio::process::Command`.

No global tracing-subscriber in tests (WO-0042 lesson): both new tests assert state via the public `state` / `error_message` / `restart_attempts` fields — NOT by capturing tracing events. Avoids cross-test contamination.

Test wall-time budget: `test_circuit_breaker` configures the manager with `with_circuit_breaker_base(Duration::from_millis(5))` so the {1, 2, 4} s production backoffs become {5, 10, 20} ms in the test — total ≤50 ms instead of 7 s. Test-runtime invariant explicitly asserted (`elapsed < Duration::from_secs(2)`).
DEC-0005 module-root test commit budget: commits 7 and 8 (the two test-introduction commits) MAY exceed the ~50 LOC soft target. Documented in scope_in commit ladder.
Logging discipline: `tracing::info!` / `warn!` only — NO `println!` / `eprintln!` (rust-style.md forbids those in library code). Target `ucil.plugin.lifecycle` aligns with master-plan §15.2.
